Was still set to /Invasive-Species/ from GitHub Pages, causing blank screen.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add video interview system: recording, panel invites, UX improvements

- Schema: add PanelInvite model, RecordingStatus enum, recording fields
  (recordingUrl, egressId, recordingStatus) and PayType to EmploymentRecord
- LiveKit: add EgressClient helpers (startRoomRecording, stopRoomRecording,
  getRecordingUrl) pointing at MinIO S3-compatible storage
- Recording API: POST routes to start/stop egress; LiveKit webhook sets
  recordingStatus READY/FAILED on egress_ended event
- Panel invites: schedule route creates signed PanelInvite tokens per
  interviewer; token route resolves invite → real participant name/identity
- Notifications: notifyPanelInterviewer triggers panel-interviewer-invited
  Novu workflow with personalised join URL
- Interview page: resolve ?token= panel invite from searchParams; derive
  displayName priority (panel > employer > candidate > guest)
- InterviewClientShell: waiting-room gate (opens 10 min before scheduled
  time with live countdown), consent screen, panel-aware join button label
- LiveKitMeeting: in-call countdown timer (shows full duration, counts down
  once connected); 5-min warning banner; grace-period overlay (60 s) when
  time expires with End Now / +10 min options; Thank You screen after end;
  auto-start recording when employer connects; auto-stop on interview end
- Playback page: renders recording video when READY, status cards otherwise

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(curation): HIGH_DIVERGENCE flag for true_edge >= 20% in curate_plays.py

DIAMOND tier (true_edge >= 20%) has the lowest win rate (51.4%) despite getting
the least scrutiny — confirmed by Lena, Sankore JW-10 rule. Flag fires inline
inside _format_player_block() and requires Claude to name a structural reason
(injury vacuum, confirmed steam, WR-confirmed scheme edge) before grading STRONG.
Default on non-specific answer: downgrade to LEAN.

EXEMPT combos (confirmed high WR at high edge): BLK UNDER, 3PM UNDER,
REB UNDER, TOV UNDER. Computed at curation time from true_edge already in
bet_recommendations — no schema change, no module_f.py change. 1 file, 12 lines.

Henrik APPROVED. Sankore HIGH_DIVERGENCE recommendation implemented.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: production-quality nonparametric tests with midranks and tie correction

Addresses reviewer feedback on correctness issues:

1. ALL rank-based tests now use midranks (fractional/averaged ranks for ties)
   instead of ordinalrank — fixes the primary correctness risk
2. Tie correction factor applied to variance in MW, Wilcoxon, and KW tests
3. Continuity correction (−0.5) in z-score computations for MW and Wilcoxon
4. Kruskal-Wallis H statistic corrected for ties: H/(1 − ΣT/(N³−N))
5. Friedman test updated to use midranks within blocks
6. NEW: permanova_multi() — sequential multi-factor PERMANOVA (Type I SS)
   supporting models like adonis2(dist ~ group + run)
7. Comprehensive test coverage: MW (with/without ties), Wilcoxon (paired,
   zero-diff edge case), KW (with ties), PERMANOVA single + multi-factor

These changes make statistease's nonparametric suite equivalent to R's
wilcox.test, kruskal.test, friedman.test, and vegan::adonis2 for the
standard use cases.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Convert clause extraction to batched CLI calls (11x token reduction)

The clause extractor was making individual CLI calls per deal — each paying
~100K tokens of system prompt overhead. This converts it to the same batched
pattern used by deal_enricher: 10 filings per CLI call, piped via stdin.

Key changes:
- ClaudeExtractor: add extract_batch_via_cli(), _build_cli_env(), JSON array
  parsing, batch telemetry. Default model changed from opus to sonnet.
- ClauseExtractionPipeline: add extract_clauses_batch() with 3-phase
  orchestration (fetch → batch → oversized fallback). QuotaGate integration
  for budget-aware processing. Configurable inter-call pacing (default 30s)
  to share subscription quota with bmc-quant and other agents.
- run_clause_extraction(): --retry, --max-per-run, --pacing CLI flags.
- research_routes.py: POST /research/clauses/run + GET /research/clauses/status
  API endpoints for triggering and monitoring clause extraction.
- prompts.py: CLAUSE_EXTRACTION_BATCH_PROMPT_WRAPPER for multi-filing output.

Token savings: ~110K per 10 deals (was ~1.2M). Estimated 11x reduction.
5,324 pending deals now feasible within subscription quota.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

test: MLP controller 13.7% + self-scheduling (WIP, charge=0 bug)

MLP controller (8->16->6 REINFORCE): 13.7%
- Learns theta=30%, reverse=21%, flip=18% but not aggressive enough
- Max 30% to one op vs fix schedule 65% = too much exploration

Self-scheduling (network votes via op neurons): WIP
- Op neurons charge always 0 after spike reset
- Need spike count readout instead of charge readout
- Pain/reward feedback mechanism designed but not functional yet

All adaptive scheduling approaches < fix schedule (23.8%):
  MLP: 13.7%, Adaptive D20: 12.5%, Combined: 10.5%
The fix schedule remains king — navigable infinity principle applies
to schedules too: simpler fixed > complex adaptive at this scale.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: update hook signatures and remove deprecated mujoco calls

Fixed hook signature warnings and MuJoCo API compatibility issues:

1. Updated hook invoke signatures to match framework interface
   - Hooks now accept all framework functions as positional arguments
   - Removed **kwargs and explicitly declared all parameters
   - Fixed: NonFallHook, OpponentPolicyHook, FallDetectionHook, UprightConstraintHook, FreezeRobotHook

2. Fixed MuJoCo 3.5.0 API compatibility
   - Removed deprecated mujoco.mj_camLight() calls
   - Camera lighting is automatic in MuJoCo 3.5+
   - This function was removed in recent MuJoCo versions

3. Added required imports at module level
   - Added: import mujoco, from scipy.spatial.transform import Rotation as R
   - Removed redundant imports from inside hook methods

All warnings eliminated. Robot stands stably at 1.4m height with zero actions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

chore: update chapter8

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: correct robot initialization and control parameters

Fixed robot behavior regression by aligning with reference implementation:

1. Fixed root height: Changed DEFAULT_ROOT_HEIGHT from 1.282 to 1.4
   - Matches the old CombatGymEnv implementation
   - Ensures robots start at correct height

2. Added dynamic action scale calculation from joint limits
   - New method: _compute_action_scale_from_joint_limits()
   - For joints with limits: scale = 0.25 * (upper - lower)
   - For joints without limits: scale = 0.25
   - This matches the old _default_action_scale_from_joint_limits()

3. Fixed reference position initialization in reset()
   - Changed from copying current joint positions to setting zeros
   - Ensures zero actions correspond to maintaining zero joint positions
   - Matches old reset_controller_config() behavior

These fixes ensure:
- Robot stands stably with zero actions (standing posture)
- Initial posture matches the reference implementation
- Non-fall mode properly prevents falling
- Residual PD control works correctly

All 20 core tests pass and examples run successfully.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
